business.headline: Sensex, Nifty end flat after wild swings as market waits for clearer signalsThe BSE Sensex closed at 78,520.30, up 26.76 points or 0.03%, while the NSE Nifty50 settled at 24,364.85, higher by 11.30 points or 0.05%.

Should you buy gold now? Here's what this Akshaya Tritiya revealedGold prices are high and markets are uncertain. Yet, this Akshaya Tritiya saw steady buying. So, should you invest now or wait? Here's what buyer trends and market signals are telling us.

Commercial vs residential investing: Which investment gives better returns?For most first-time investors, the confusion is simple. Should you buy a house for steady rent or invest in a shop or office for higher returns? The answer is not straightforward, because both options behave very differently in terms of income, risk and effort.

Why 71% of consumers believe loyalty rewards don't actually foster loyaltyLoyalty has always been about more than transactions – it is about the feeling of being valued. Customers stay not because of points but because a brand makes them feel recognised in ways that matter.

What is Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool? Rs 13,000 crore plan explainedWhy has the government launched the Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool with Rs 13,000 crore? The answer lies in rising war risks, costly shipping routes and protecting trade from global shocks.

DA hiked by 2%: How much extra will you get and how to calculate?The government has raised Dearness Allowance by 2%, but what does it really mean for your monthly salary? Here's a simple look at how much extra you can expect and how the calculation works.

Delhi power bills may rise after tribunal rejects plea on Rs 30,000 crore duesThe dues are linked to pending recoveries payable to Delhi's power distribution companies (discoms) under a larger liquidation plan aimed at clearing long-standing liabilities in the power sector.

ICICI Bank vs HDFC Bank after Q4: Which stock looks like a better buy now?ICICI Bank gained after Q4 results while HDFC Bank slipped, raising a key investor question. Which private banking giant now offers better growth, dividends and value potential? Read on to find out.

Big relief for shipping industry: India clears Rs 13,000 crore insurance poolIndia's shipping sector has long depended on global insurance markets to keep goods moving across oceans. But recent disruptions in key trade routes and rising geopolitical risks have exposed a critical gap.

ICICI Bank rises nearly 2% after Q4 results: Should you buy now?ICICI Bank reported a net profit of about Rs 13,702 crore for the fourth quarter, up 8.5% from a year ago. It also announced a dividend of Rs 12 per share for FY26, higher than the Rs 11 per share declared last year, adding to investor confidence.

HDFC Bank drops 1% after Q4 results: Profit rises, so why is stock down?HDFC Bank reported a net profit of about Rs 19,221 crore for the fourth quarter, up around 9% from a year ago. But shares of India's largest private lender fell in early trade.

Strait of Hormuz crisis returns: Oil prices climb 6% on fresh conflict fearsLatest data shows WTI crude at $88.98, up 6.12%, while Brent crude has climbed to $95.23, up 5.37% as of 9 am. The sudden rise reflects growing concern that oil supply could be hit if tensions continue.

Gold falls Rs 1,700, silver down nearly Rs 5,000: Should you buy now or wait?Gold and silver prices fell sharply on Monday, offering some relief to buyers after the recent rally pushed rates near record highs across retail and futures markets in India.
Sensex, Nifty erase early losses to trade higher amid high volatilityThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 168.96 points to 78,662.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 21,05 points to 24,374.60 as of 9:30 am.
Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,469.50 at 7:57 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Friday's close of 24,353.55.

Why your car insurance may not pay when you need it mostAn accident, a flooded road, a damaged engine, and a car insurance claim is filed with the expectation that the insurer will step in. But for many, that moment turns into something else entirely: confusion, partial payouts, or in some cases, outright rejection.

India's digital payments boom has a growing fraud problemIndia's digital boom has made payments effortless, but also easier to exploit. From fake identities to instant scams, fraudsters are moving faster than the systems meant to stop them, and users are paying the price.

Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

On paper, compliant: How labour laws are quietly underminedIn the third episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how employers maintain compliance on paper while systematically evading legal obligations in practice.

Digital gold, ETFs or SGBs: Where should you invest this Akshaya Tritiya?Akshaya Tritiya is here, and gold buying is once again in focus. But instead of just buying jewellery, many are now exploring smarter ways to invest. So, which option, digital gold, ETFs or SGBs, makes the most sense?

entertainment.headline: Beef Season 2 review: Plays it safe, loses the bite that made Season 1 addictiveRelease Date:Thu Apr 16, 2026 Beef Season 2 series review: Beef Season 2 presents a quieter, more introspective follow-up to the intense and chaotic first season, focusing on simmering resentments and complex relationships rather than explosive drama.

Drishyam 3 director reacts to TV serial-like execution jibe, defends his choices Mollywood director Jeethu Joseph responded to criticism over his films' technical quality and lighting style. He revealed that Drishyam 3 will have a fresh narrative and release on May 21.

Ramayana sparks global blockbuster talk at CinemaCon: Exhibitor weighs in Global exhibitor John Fithian expressed that Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana could become India's first global blockbuster. Early footage screened at CinemaCon 2026 has sparked strong worldwide buzz.

Vijay blesses newlyweds with gift during political rally. Watch the Video Vijay blessed newlyweds with a gift during an election rally. The actor was campaigning for his party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), ahead of the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections.

Yash shares Toxic story details, says it captures darkest emotions, dilemmas Yash shared new insights into Toxic: A Fairy Tale for Grown-Ups, revealing its layered storytelling and emotionally intense core. The film, he said, promises more than just action, exploring deeper human dilemmas in a unique way.

Ajay Devgn, Kajol shower love on daughter Nysa on her birthday, share rare pics Ajay Devgn and Kajol celebrated daughter Nysa Devgan's 23rd birthday with heartfelt throwback posts and loving messages. The couple shared childhood memories and wishes, making the occasion special for their daughter.

Days of Our Lives actor Patrick Muldoon dies at 57 after suffering heart attack Patrick Muldoon, best known for Days of Our Lives, died at the age of 57 after suffering a heart attack. The actor had a long career in television and films, and was also active as a producer and musician.

Anushka-Virat seek blessings from Premanand Maharaj on Akshaya Tritiya. Video Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli were spotted at a Vrindavan ashram, and videos from their visit are now going viral. The couple's spiritual side and recent public moments have once again caught attention.

Vijay-Sangeetha divorce case: Family court orders couple to attend hearing in June A Chengalpet family court has set June 15 as the date for Vijay and Sangeetha's divorce hearing following serious allegations. Sangeetha filed for divorce in February.

LiverDoc backs Malayalam film Vaazha 2, calls it 'public health activism' amid row In his recent social media post, Abby Philips aka LiverDoc, urged Vaazha 2 makers not to alter the film after legal pushback from the Ayurveda body.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do teaser: Ayushmann caught in chaos with Sara, Wamiqa and Rakul Ayushmann Khurrana is caught in chaos with Sara Ali Khan, Wamiqa Gabbi and Rakul Preet Singh, in his upcoming film, Pati Patni Aur Woh Do. The teaser of the upcoming film offers a glimpse into the laughter and chaos.

Rishab Shetty reviews Youth, calls 24-year-old director Ken an asset to cinema Kannada actor-director Rishab Shetty praised the Tamil film Youth for its touching tribute to parents and excellent performances. The film has garnered critical acclaim and commercial success, streaming now on Netflix.

Dhurandhar's Rakesh Bedi exposes Bollywood's harsh hierarchy: Money was for the hero Rakesh Bedi opened up about a moment from his early career that revealed Bollywood's power dynamics and hierarchy. The veteran actor also reflected on how the scale of stardom has transformed over the years.

It's a girl: Director Atlee, wife Priya welcome second child Filmmaker Atlee and wife Priya have welcomed their second child, a baby girl, bringing joy to the family. They shared the news with a cute cartoon portrait of their son, Meer, celebrating his new role as big brother.

Samay Raina says backlash left him fearing debt like Amitabh Bachchan Comedian Samay Raina revealed how the backlash from India's Got Latent controversy left him fearing bankruptcy, similar to Amitabh Bachchan's financial crisis in the late 90s.

Dhanush teases Vada Chennai 2 update with promise of a powerful return. Watch Actor Dhanush has revealed that the sequel to Vada Chennai will go on floors soon, promising a powerful continuation of the original crime drama. Directed by Vetri Maaran and produced by Ishari K Ganesh, the film is highly anticipated by fans.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 32: Ranveer's film Rs 53 crore away from Baahubali 2 Dhurandhar 2 continues its record-breaking box office run, slowly moving closer to Baahubali 2 on the global charts. Here's a look at its latest numbers, rankings, and the challenge ahead.

Dipika Kakar says crying eases anxiety, shares update on cancer treatment Dipika Kakar opened up about dealing with anxiety during MRI scans amid her cancer treatment, sharing that crying helps her cope. The actor continues her recovery with close monitoring after her latest scan and is set to begin immunotherapy.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 3: Rs 100 crore a cakewalk for Akshay's film worldwide Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla is off to a strong start at the box office, racing towards the Rs 100 crore mark within its opening weekend. Here's how the horror-comedy performed day-wise and worldwide.

general news.headline: Don't turn back: Japan braces for 3-metre tsunami waves after powerful quakeFootage aired on NHK showed ships sailing out of Hachinohe port in Hokkaido in anticipation of the waves as a 'Tsunami! Evacuate!' alert flashed across the screen. A 3-metre tsunami could cause damage to low-lying areas, flooding buildings, and anybody exposed would be caught in its currents, according to JMA.

US blockade of Iran ports hurdle to talks: Asim Munir to Trump on phone callDuring a telephonic conversation with President Trump, Pakistan's Asim Munir flagged the US blockade of Iranian ports as an obstacle to talks with Iran. Trump said he would look into the advice.

The miracle at India's petrol pumpsWith oil on fire due to the war in the Middle East, all big economies have raised petrol and diesel prices. However, pump prices of normal petrol and diesel in India have stayed unchanged since May 2022. This is what's behind this miracle.

How a Chinese satellite may have tracked US bases before Iran strikesDocuments reviewed by the Financial Times indicated that the satellite acquired by Iran was developed and launched by Earth Eye Co, a Chinese company that provides "in-orbit delivery" services, where satellites are transferred to international clients after being placed in orbit.

How Gulf countries paid $142 billion to US for a leaky THAAD umbrellaGulf countries poured billions into American missile defences designed to stop Iran's arsenal. Yet when retaliation came, sheer volume and cost asymmetry turned the equation. The Middle East countries were left with charred THAAD systems and a costly lesson.

US wanted to kill all of us: IRIS Dena sailor on torpedo attack in Indian OceanAn Iranian sailor who survived the US attack on the IRIS Dena warship in the Indian Ocean has recounted the ordeal and accused the US of planning to kill the crew. The IRIS Dena was returning to Iran after a naval exercise in India when it was torpedoed by the US near Sri Lanka.

Alone by the boundary ropes, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in tears after RR's loss to KKRIPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals' teenage superstar Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was spotted in tears near the boundary rope after their loss to Kolkata Knight Riders at Eden Gardens. The youngster's 28-ball 46 went in vain as KKR restricted Rajasthan to 155 before successfully chasing the target.

Private jet crashes into hillside in Chhattisgarh, smoke and flames seen at spotA private aircraft crashed into a hillside in Chhattisgarh's Jashpur district. The jet went down after hitting a tree in a forested area, locals said. Casualties are feared as authorities probe the incident.

The legend of the white towel on sarkari kursiThe white towel on government office chairs may look like an everyday thing, but it carries a long history of colonial habits, heat, hygiene and hierarchy. Here is how a simple cloth became one of India's most recognisable symbols of official power.

Live: 10 Indian ships crossed Strait of Hormuz amid blockade, says govt officialUS-Iran-Israel War Live Updates: Iran refused to hold the second round of the ceasefire talks with the United States, citing Washington's "excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts in stance, repeated contradictions, and the naval blockade of the maritime chokepoint, Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Donald Trump said that the US delegation team, led by Vice President JD Vance, will arrive in Islamabad today. However, after Iran's refusal, further ceasefire talks regarding the prevailing West Asia conflict look bleak. Amid the developments, Pakistan's Prime Minister, Shehbaz Sharif, held a phone call with Iran's President, Masoud Pezeshkian, and discussed the regional situation. He also briefed Pezeshkian on the conversation the South Asian country had with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Namita Thapar hits back after being trolled for reel on health benefits of namazNamita Thapar has hit back with a strongly worded video after facing online trolling and abuse over her Instagram reel on the health benefits of namaz.

India shares fresh advisory for tankers in Hormuz after firing on 2 Indian ships Live UpdatesIndia has issued a fresh advisory to Indian-flagged vessels in the Persian Gulf, asking them to stay clear of Larak Island and transit only when directed by it to ensure safety. Sources told India Today that the Indian Navy is prioritising security for all Indian vessels waiting to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

Video: Noida woman confronts drunk man harassing Muslim couple, accused arrestedAccording to police, the situation escalated when the man questioned the couple's identity and got into a verbal altercation with them. The couple had reportedly asked him to stop drinking in the open, which led to the confrontation.

Telugu duo in US face 5-year jail for H-1B fraud; hired people for ghost jobsTwo Indian-origin Telugu men in California admitted to committing H1-B visa fraud. The duo hired people for jobs at the University of California that never existed. They now face 5 years in jail in the US.

Amid US's Pak tilt, India-Russia pact on troop, warship deployment comes into forceThe India-Russia RELOS agreement, which allows deployment of troops, warships and aircraft in each other's territory even in conflict scenarios, has come into force. The operationalisation of the pact comes at a time the Trump White House is increasingly leaning towards Pakistan and its army chief, Asim Munir.

7.5-magnitude earthquake hits Japan. What's happening beneath the surface?Japan is one of the most earthquake-prone places on Earth, accounting for 10% of all tremors worldwide despite making up just 0.3% of the planet's total land mass.

Ghaziabad woman shares Rapido driver's inappropriate messages, company reactsA woman from Ghaziabad has shared screenshots of a WhatsApp conversation allegedly initiated by the Rapido bike driver who had dropped her.

Oh my god: Passengers cry, panic as Fly91 flight faces mid-air scareThe incident caused anxiety among passengers as the aircraft remained airborne for nearly three hours. Some passengers were visibly distressed, with a few reportedly in tears and praying during the prolonged delay.

Iran to skip talks 2.0 in Pak, blames excessive US demands, Hormuz blockadeHopes of a breakthrough faded after Iran confirmed its absence from the second round of talks in Islamabad, which it said stemmed from Washington's "excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts in stance, repeated contradictions", and the naval blockade of its ports.

Congress blames PM for Pak's new branding: 'Dalal' country hosting Iran talks 2.0The Congress accused the BJP government of a diplomatic failure over Pakistan hosting US-Iran talks and Asim Munir's growing proximity to Donald Trump.

Vercel hacked, hacker using ShinyHunters name to sell data for $2 millionVercel, a cloud platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was recently compromised in a cyberattack stemming from an AI tool. The company says that no sensitive data was accessed after hackers claimed to be selling Vercel customer data online. Vercel has clients such as OpenAI, Cursor, Bose, and Pinterest.

Drop the matter, arrested TCS Nashik manager told complainant in sex abuse caseRecalling her ordeal, the survivor alleged that, despite multiple verbal complaints against accused Raza Memon, no concrete action was taken. Instead, she claimed, senior officials, including an attempt to silence her concerns.

Feeling hot? Here's when the first heatwave of 2026 will hit DelhiNorth India braces for an intense heatwave as temperatures soar to 42–45 degrees Celsius across Delhi-NCR and neighbouring states, with the IMD warning of early extreme summer conditions and rising health risks.

Unlike 9/11, Cold War, no windfall now: Pak bleeds as Iran-US crisis deepensHistorically, Pakistan's strategic location brought economic gains, with wars and global alignments yielding grants, debt relief, and reimbursements. But that equation is now reversing, as Gulf tensions trigger capital flight, reserve depletion, and rising repayment pressures.

sports.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is one for the future: Shreyas praises 'spectacular' RR star Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer has heaped praise on Rajasthan Royals' young star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, describing him as a "spectacular talent" and predicting a bright future after his impressive run in IPL 2026.

Red flag for Indian athletics: India placed in highest-risk doping bracket India has been moved to the highest-risk 'Category A' bracket for doping after consistently recording the most violations globally, forcing the World Athletics body's Athletics Integrity Unit to impose stringent new testing protocols.

IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma switches to Hitman mode in MI nets ahead of GT clash IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma returned to nets ahead of MI's clash with Gujarat Titans, raising hopes of a comeback. With Mumbai Indians struggling, his return could be the spark needed to revive their IPL 2026 campaign.

Hardik, when was your last match-winning knock? MI skipper urged to step up IPL 2026: Hardik Pandya faces mounting pressure as Mumbai Indians struggle to find winning momentum this season. Aakash Chopra questioned the skipper's lack of a recent match-winning knock, urging him to step up.

Aren't you ashamed? Ex-KKR star slams classist trolling of Rinku Singh IPL 2026: Former India opener Aakash Chopra said he was delighted for Rinku Singh after his match-winning knock for Kolkata Knight Riders against Rajasthan Royals, adding that the batter had faced classist trolling on social media.

Decide on posthumous Padma Vibhushan for KD Jadhav by May 4: High Court to Centre Wrestler Khashaba Jadhav, who passed away in 1984, had won the bronze medal in the Helsinki Olympic Games held in 1952. He was awarded the Arjuna Award posthumously in 2001.

Riyan Parag will make his comeback: RR coach backs skipper despite lean patch IPL 2026: Riyan Parag continues to endure a lean IPL 2026 run, but Rajasthan Royals remain firmly behind their skipper. Batting coach Vikram Rathour insists it is only a matter of time before he turns it around.

Who is Yash Raj Punja? Ex-UAE cricketer's brother impresses on IPL debut IPL 2026, KKR vs RR: Yash Raj Punja impressed on his IPL debut for Rajasthan Royals, returning with figures of 4-0-25-1 against Kolkata Knight Riders on Sunday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Alone by the boundary ropes, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in tears after RR's loss to KKR IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals' teenage superstar Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was spotted in tears near the boundary rope after their loss to Kolkata Knight Riders at Eden Gardens. The youngster's 28-ball 46 went in vain as KKR restricted Rajasthan to 155 before successfully chasing the target.

Mikel Arteta sticks to 'I believe' as Arsenal's loss to City tightens title race Gunners manager Mikel Arteta stuck to his "I believe" message despite Arsenal's loss to Manchester City tightening the title race. With just five games left, the pressure is now firmly on both sides.

Forged in Chennai, unleashed in Hyderabad: Inside Praful Hinge and Sakib's rise IPL 2026: The MRF Pace Foundation in Chennai continues to shape India's fast-bowling future, with its rigorous system producing talents like Praful Hinge and Sakib Hussain, now making their mark for SunRisers Hyderabad.

Pressure mounts on Hardik Pandya as MI's ship faces stormy Ahmedabad test vs GTIPL 2026, GT vs MI: Mumbai Indians have lost four games on the trot and will be eager to return to winning ways against Gujarat Titans at a venue where they are yet to register a victory over them.

Saw KKR dressing room in tears, many made baseless judgements about us: ChakravarthyIPL 2026, KKR vs RR: Varun Chakravarthy revealed emotional scenes in KKR's dressing room after their first IPL 2026 win, crediting team resilience amid criticism. Varun's match-winning spell and Rinku Singh's heroics sealed a dramatic chase against Rajasthan Royals in Kolkata on Sunday.

Roman Reigns beats CM Punk as WrestleMania 42 Night 2 delivers chaosRoman Reigns defeated CM Punk in a clean main event to win the World Heavyweight title. WrestleMania 42 Night 2 also saw multiple title changes and a potential farewell moment for Brock Lesnar.

Lalit Modi claims IPL team owner did black magic after bizarre SRH vs CSK episodeIPL 2026: A viral 'black magic' controversy involving Chennai Super Kings and Sunrisers Hyderabad was dismissed as fake by Lalit Modi. However, his remarks hinted that such bizarre claims may not be entirely new in IPL history.

IPL Play Of The Day: Pant goes full Ghajini as PBKS plunder LSG in MullanpurIPL 2026: It was a night to forget for Rishabh Pant, the LSG skipper, as his decision to not use main spinner M. Siddharth effectively came to haunt them the match against PBKS. Pant also had a howler with DRS, which gave Cooper Connolly a second life.

Happy ending: Ricky Ponting hugs Shashank Singh, moves past anger over fielding lapsesIPL 2026, PBKS vs LSG Ricky Ponting shared a warm hug with Shashank Singh after the match, putting aside his earlier frustration over the batter's costly fielding lapses. On Sunday, Punjab Kings beat the Lucknow Super Giants by 54 runs.

Hit most sixes, win my bat: Shreyas Iyer on PBKS challenge after LSG smash showShreyas Iyer revealed Punjab Kings have an in-house six-hitting contest, with his bat as the prize, after PBKS smashed 21 sixes against LSG in IPL 2026. PBKS have 63 sixes in six matches so far in the IPL.

Unbeaten, unbroken, unbowed: Priyansh, Connolly power Punjab to epic thrashing of LSGIPL 2026, PBKS vs LSG: Priyansh Arya and Cooper Connolly powered Punjab Kings to a massive 54-run win over Lucknow Super Giants. As Punjab continued their unbeaten run, Lucknow Super Giants sank further in the Indian Premier League table.

Advantage Manchester City in title race as Haaland's winner downs ArsenalManchester City defeated Arsenal at the Etihad as goals from Erling Haaland and Rayan Cherki sealed a huge win in the Premier League title race despite Kai Havertz's equaliser.

Babar Azam upskilling in T20s? Pakistan batter plays only 1 dot ball in PSL centuryPSL 2026: Babar Azam smashed a 52-ball century against Quetta Gladiators, playing just one dot ball in the entire innings. He brought up his hundred with six fours and four sixes, powering Peshawar Zalmi to a commanding 255 for three.

Rohit Sharma returns to MI nets, but will he play against GT in Ahmedabad?Rohit Sharma returned to the nets ahead of the Mumbai Indians' clash against the Gujarat Titans after missing the PBKS game with a hamstring injury. A final decision on his availability is pending.

Priyansh Arya, Connolly headline Mullanpur rout of Lucknow with 182-run standThe Punjab Kings turned Mullanpur into a batting amphitheater on Sunday, as Priyansh Arya (93) and Cooper Connolly (87) stitched together a breathtaking 182-run stand to dismantle Lucknow Super Giants in their IPL 2026 clash.

Relieved Ajinkya Rahane jokes of getting 5 extra minutes after KKR's win over RRA relieved Ajinkya Rahane joked that he was happy to get the five extra minutes to conduct the post-match interview after KKR secured their first win of the season against RR.

India Women's familiar frailties exposed again as South Africa go 2-0 up in DurbanSAW vs INDW, 2nd T20I: India Women lost the second T20I by eight wickets on Sunday at the Kingsmead in Durban to go 0-2 down in the five-match series. Chasing 148, South Africa romped home with 17 balls to spare.

Rashid Khan turned down citizenship offer from India: I will play for AfghanistanAfghanistan talisman Rashid Khan has revealed that he turned down offers of citizenship from India and Australia, choosing instead to remain committed to representing his country.

How Rinku Singh overcame early brainfade moment to power KKR to first winRinku Singh revealed he had a brief mental lapse during his match-winning knock of 53 against Rajasthan Royals as KKR secured their first IPL 2026 win at Eden Gardens. Rinku finally came into form with his knock of 53.

Rinku the difference as KKR squeeze through for first win in Eden tug-of-warIPL 2026, KKR vs RR: Rinku Singh held his nerve in a tense finish to guide Kolkata Knight Riders to a much-needed first win of IPL 2026, edging past Rajasthan Royals in a low-scoring thriller at Eden Gardens.

Best stumping of IPL 2026? Dhruv Jurel's electric reaction stuns Cameron GreenIPL 2026, KKR vs RR: Dhruv Jurel pulled off a stupendous stumping to dismiss Cameron Green, who made 27 off 13 in the match on Sunday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Varun Chakravarthy back in form, becomes fastest Indian spinner to 200 T20 wicketsVarun Chakravarthy returned to form with a decisive spell as Kolkata Knight Riders' spin attack restricted Rajasthan Royals to 155/9 in Match 28 of the Indian Premier League at Eden Gardens on Sunday, becoming the fastest Indian spinner to reach 200 T20 wickets in the process.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
